prevalence.value: common; multiple episodes per child-year in early childhood in high-risk settings
prevalence.source: WHO/UNICEF + Indian child health program context
prevalence.trend: decreasing mortality with ORS/zinc scale-up; high morbidity persists

incidence.value: 2-4 episodes per child-year (higher in high-burden districts)

burden.dalyPerCapita: high per-case; major contributor to child morbidity and some mortality
burden.mortalityRate: declining but still significant in vulnerable districts

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical dehydration assessment per WHO; stool testing reserved for severe/bloody/persistent cases

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Variable; improved with ORS/zinc and timely referral for severe dehydration

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Primary care and community health worker assessment focused on dehydration and danger signs
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: ORS + zinc, continued feeding; IV fluids for severe dehydration; antibiotics only for dysentery/cholera suspicion
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: WASH gaps, malnutrition, and limited access to facilities during severe dehydration
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Large-scale ORS/zinc programs and rotavirus vaccination scale-up in many states
